parts: I'm trying to figure out our pricing. We have a B2B SaaS product for HR teams. Right now we're thinking somewhere between $50 and $200 per seat per month. We have about 8 pilot customers paying nothing. Our burn rate is $40k/month and we have 6 months of runway left.

Given your constraints, I'd recommend moving toward a value-based pricing model rather than cost-plus. For HR SaaS at your stage, $80-120/seat/month is a common anchor for SMB. But before setting a number, have you tested willingness to pay with your pilots? A simple 'what would you pay' conversation with 3 pilots would tell you more than any benchmark.

We haven't done formal WTP interviews. Two pilots have mentioned $100/seat feels reasonable, one said they'd need budget approval above $75. We're targeting companies with 50-500 employees. I think we need to decide before end of month because we're onboarding a paying customer next week.

Given the $75 threshold you heard and your urgency, I'd suggest launching at $85/seat/month with an annual discount to $75 (effectively the sensitive threshold but framed as a reward for commitment). This keeps you above the $75 single-mention veto while staying below $100. You can always raise prices later — it's much harder to lower them without signaling desperation.

That makes sense. One more thing — should we do per-seat or flat fee? A flat fee for up to 100 seats might be simpler to sell. Maybe $4,000/month for unlimited seats up to 100, then $30/seat above that.

Flat fee is easier to sell but harder to expand revenue. Per-seat naturally grows as the customer grows. For your stage, per-seat wins — it aligns your revenue with customer success, and HR teams almost always track headcount anyway so the metric is intuitive. Flat fee tends to benefit customers who expand fast and punishes you for their growth.
Ok, we're going with $85/seat/month, per-seat pricing. Annual plan at $75/seat. We'll review pricing again after 10 paying customers.

title: Should we hire a VP Sales now?

parts: We have $240k ARR, 6 months runway, and I (the CEO) am doing all the sales. A recruiter reached out about a VP Sales candidate who wants $180k base + equity. I'm torn — do we hire now or wait?

$180k base on $240k ARR with 6 months runway is extremely high risk. The general rule is: don't hire a VP Sales until you have repeatable sales motion proof — meaning you've closed 10+ deals yourself and can write down exactly what you say and do. Have you hit that bar?
I've closed 8 deals. The process isn't fully documented but I know what works. I'm worried if I don't hire now I'll lose the candidate and stay stuck as the only seller forever.

The fear of losing one candidate is a known psychological trap. Great VP Sales candidates exist continuously. The risk of hiring at $180k base with 6 months runway is existential — one bad quarter and you're done. My recommendation: pass on this candidate, close 2 more deals yourself, document the process, then hire an AE at $80k who can follow your playbook. A VP Sales who has to build the playbook themselves at your stage often fails anyway.
You're right. I think I was just tired of selling. We'll pass on this VP and hire an AE instead once we have 12 customers. That gives us a proven playbook and more runway comfort.
